# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for our complete application stack (VPC, EC2, RDS, S3, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure components. We'll add CloudWatch alarms and detailed monitoring after we deploy and verify the basic setup works.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud monitoring and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: us-west-2a

  PrivateSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.2.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: us-west-2b

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC

  RouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref RouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      RouteTableId: !Ref RouteTable

  SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Allow SSH and HTTP access
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0
      InstanceType: t2.micro
      KeyName: my-key-pair
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref SecurityGroup

  RDSInstance:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Properties:
      Engine: mysql
      EngineVersion: 5.7
      DBInstanceClass: db.t2.micro
      AllocatedStorage: 20
      MasterUsername: admin
      MasterUserPassword: SecurePass123!
      VPCSecurityGroups:
        - !Ref SecurityGroup

  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: my-application-bucket

  LoadBalancer:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer
    Properties:
      Subnets:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref SecurityGroup

Outputs:
  VPCId:
    Description: The ID of the VPC
    Value: !Ref VPC
  PublicSubnetId:
    Description: The ID of the public subnet
    Value: !Ref PublicSubnet
  PrivateSubnetId:
    Description: The ID of the private subnet
    Value: !Ref PrivateSubnet
  EC2InstanceId:
    Description: The ID of the EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  RDSInstanceId:
    Description: The ID of the RDS instance
    Value: !Ref RDSInstance
  S3BucketName:
    Description: The name of the S3 bucket
    Value: !Ref S3Bucket
  LoadBalancerDnsName:
    Description: The DNS name of the load balancer
    Value: !GetAtt LoadBalancer.DNSName
